creator: aryeh.friedman@gmail.com
text:
Yes the message is very possible because it is what I get and I have all the patches you suggested and the full libressl 2.9.1 built from source:

aryehl@lilith:/home/aryehl/Desktop% cd /usr/ports/lang/rust
aryehl@lilith:/usr/ports/lang/rust% cd files/
aryehl@lilith:ports/lang/rust/files% ls
config.toml
patch-src_bootstrap_bootstrap.py
patch-src_bootstrap_native.rs
patch-src_librustc__llvm_build.rs
patch-src_librustc__target_spec_armv6__unknown__freebsd.rs
patch-src_librustc__target_spec_armv7__unknown__freebsd.rs
patch-src_librustc__target_spec_i686__unknown__freebsd.rs
patch-src_librustc__target_spec_mod.rs
patch-src_libstd_sys_unix_stack__overflow.rs
patch-src_llvm-project_llvm_utils_llvm-build_llvmbuild_main.py
patch-src_stdsimd_crates_std__detect_src_detect_os_aarch64.rs
patch-src_stdsimd_crates_std__detect_src_detect_os_freebsd_arm.rs
patch-src_stdsimd_crates_std__detect_src_detect_os_freebsd_auxvec.rs
patch-src_stdsimd_crates_std__detect_src_detect_os_freebsd_mod.rs
patch-src_stdsimd_crates_std__detect_src_detect_os_freebsd_powerpc.rs
patch-vendor_backtrace-sys_src_libbacktrace_fileline.c
patch-vendor_backtrace_src_backtrace_libunwind.rs
patch-vendor_compiler__builtins_build.rs
patch-vendor_libc_src_unix_bsd_freebsdlike_dragonfly_mod.rs
patch-vendor_libc_src_unix_bsd_freebsdlike_freebsd_aarch64.rs
patch-vendor_libc_src_unix_bsd_freebsdlike_freebsd_arm.rs
patch-vendor_libc_src_unix_bsd_freebsdlike_freebsd_mod.rs
patch-vendor_libc_src_unix_bsd_freebsdlike_freebsd_powerpc64.rs
patch-vendor_libc_src_unix_bsd_freebsdlike_freebsd_x86.rs
patch-vendor_libc_src_unix_bsd_freebsdlike_freebsd_x86__64.rs
patch-vendor_libc_src_unix_bsd_freebsdlike_mod.rs
patch-vendor_openssl-sys_build_cfgs.rs
patch-vendor_openssl-sys_src_crypto.rs
patch-vendor_openssl-sys_src_ssl.rs
patch-vendor_openssl_build.rs
patch-vendor_openssl_src_ssl_mod.rs
patch-vendor_openssl_src_version.rs
patch-vendor_rustc-ap-rustc__target_spec_armv6__unknown__freebsd.rs
patch-vendor_rustc-ap-rustc__target_spec_armv7__unknown__freebsd.rs
patch-vendor_rustc-ap-rustc__target_spec_mod.rs
aryehl@lilith:ports/lang/rust/files% pwd
/usr/ports/lang/rust/files
aryehl@lilith:ports/lang/rust/files% pkg info libressl
libressl-2.9.1
Name           : libressl
Version        : 2.9.1
Installed on   : Sat May 11 17:57:09 2019 EDT
Origin         : security/libressl
Architecture   : FreeBSD:11:amd64
Prefix         : /usr/local
Categories     : security devel
Licenses       : BSD4CLAUSE
Maintainer     : brnrd@FreeBSD.org
WWW            : http://www.libressl.org/
Comment        : Free version of the SSL/TLS protocol forked from OpenSSL
Options        :
	MAN3           : on
	NC             : on
Shared Libs provided:
	libcrypto.so.45
	libtls.so.19
	libssl.so.47
Annotations    :
	FreeBSD_version: 1102000
	cpe            : cpe:2.3:a:openbsd:libressl:2.9.1:::::freebsd11:x64
Flat size      : 9.53MiB
Description    :
LibreSSL is an open-source implementation of the Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) and
Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocols. It was forked from the OpenSSL
cryptographic software library in April 2014 as a response by OpenBSD
developers to the Heartbleed security vulnerability in OpenSSL,
with the aim of refactoring the OpenSSL code so as to provide a more secure
implementation.

LibreSSL was forked from the OpenSSL library starting with the 1.0.1g branch
and will follow the security guidelines used elsewhere in the OpenBSD project.

WWW: http://www.libressl.org/
